title: undefined
subtitle: Not assigned yet
goal: Learn what undefined means in JavaScript.
explanation: undefined means a value has not been assigned yet.
If you create a variable without a value, JavaScript automatically assigns undefined to it.
Unlike null, undefined does not need to be written manually.
This means the variable exists but does not contain anything yet.
undefined is common while writing and debugging programs.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine an empty box that nothing has been put into yet. Nobody has even decided what should go inside. That is undefined.

remember: undefined means a value has not been set yet.
undefined and null are different values.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Many beginners think undefined and null mean the same thing. In reality, they serve different purposes.

quiz.question: When does JavaScript automatically return undefined?
options: When a variable is created without a value
